---
title: "Pickled Onion Recipe"
author: "Chris Kornaros"
date: 2026-03-06
categories: ["Pickling and Fermenting"]
---

**Source:**
[[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ifRpwe3wiJU&ab_channel=NOTANOTHERCOOKINGSHOW]{.underline}](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ifRpwe3wiJU&ab_channel=NOTANOTHERCOOKINGSHOW)

1.  Slice red onion

2.  Put slices in a colander, sitting in a larger bowl, then pour
    boiling water over them and let soak for about 1 to 1.5 minutes

3.  1.5 Cups of hot water

4.  1.5 Cups of White Vinegar

5.  2 tablespoons kosher salt

6.  2 tablespoons sugar

7.  Then mix the above until everything dissolves

**Notes:**

-   Most important thing: sugar, the sweetness is the difference

    -   Add a little more sugar than salt think 1.1:1 or so

<!-- -->

-   8oz jar holds about half a medium red onion and about half of the
    brine
